title: Osiris Glasses: Brand Overview, Styles, and Where to Buy
description: Osiris glasses offer trendy, affordable frames for young adults. See popular styles, pricing in Canada, quality details, and where to find them.
tldr: Osiris is a budget-to-mid-range eyewear brand carried primarily by Specsavers and select optical chains. Frames typically cost $100-$200 and target younger wearers with modern, trend-forward designs. Quality is decent for the price point — expect standard acetate and metal construction. Availability in Canada depends on your local optical retailer.

definition.text: An eyewear brand offering fashion-forward prescription frames at budget-to-mid-range price points. Known for trend-driven designs that appeal to younger adults. Distributed primarily through optical retail chains rather than luxury boutiques.

sections.content:
Osiris is an in-house eyewear brand developed for optical retail chains, most notably Specsavers. Unlike licensed brands (where a fashion house lends its name to frames made by an eyewear manufacturer), Osiris is designed and produced specifically for retail distribution at accessible price points.

The brand positions itself between basic house-brand frames and mid-range designer labels. You get more style variety and trendier designs than generic frames, but at a lower price than Ray-Ban, Calvin Klein, or other licensed brands.

Osiris frames appeal primarily to younger adults (18-35) who want current styles without spending $300+ on frames alone.

This is one of the most common questions that comes up during eye exams, and the answer often depends on individual factors that only a professional assessment can evaluate. What works for one person may not be ideal for another — your prescription, daily activities, facial anatomy, and budget all play a role in determining the best approach.

If you're in the Edmonton area, Charm Optical offers comprehensive eye exams that go beyond just measuring your prescription. Their optometrists assess your overall eye health, discuss your visual needs, and recommend lens and frame options tailored to your lifestyle. With direct insurance billing for most Alberta providers and same-day glasses for many prescriptions, getting the right eyewear doesn't have to be complicated or expensive. Book online — appointments are usually available within a few days.

Osiris collections rotate with fashion trends, but common style categories include:

  • Oversized round: On-trend frames with large circular or soft-round lenses. Popular in acetate with bold colours.
  • Cat-eye: Angular and vintage-inspired. Available in both subtle and dramatic versions.
  • Thin metal: Minimalist wire frames in gold, silver, and rose gold tones. Lightweight and understated.
  • Chunky rectangular: Bold acetate rectangles. The "statement frame" category.
  • Combination (combi): Metal fronts with acetate temples, or acetate fronts with metal details. A popular hybrid look.

Colour options lean toward current trends — tortoiseshell, matte black, translucent frames, and bold solids. If you're looking for more classic or conservative styles, other brands in the same price range or established designer frames might be a better fit.

The best way to evaluate frame styles is to try them on in person. Photos and online try-on tools give a rough idea, but they can't replicate how a frame feels on your face — the weight distribution, the temple pressure, and how the nose bridge sits. These comfort factors matter more than aesthetics for glasses you'll wear 12+ hours a day.

Charm Optical in Edmonton carries over 500 frames from brands including Ray-Ban, Oakley, Versace, Tom Ford, Burberry, Prada, and Dolce & Gabbana. Their opticians help you narrow down the options based on your face shape, prescription requirements (especially important for progressives), and personal style preferences. With same-day service for many prescriptions, you can walk in and walk out with new glasses the same day.

At the $100-$200 price point, expectations should be calibrated accordingly:

  • Materials: Standard acetate and stainless steel — the same base materials used in more expensive frames, though the grade and finishing may differ.
  • Hinges: Mix of spring hinges and standard barrel hinges. Spring hinges add flexibility and durability; barrel hinges are simpler but adequate.
  • Finish quality: Decent paint and polish. You won't see the hand-finishing or multi-layer acetate construction found in premium brands, but for daily wear they hold up well.
  • Durability: Expect 1-3 years of regular use before showing significant wear. More careful handling extends this.

For comparison, brands like Ray-Ban ($200-$400) offer noticeably better hinge construction, acetate layering, and long-term durability. But if you change frames frequently to follow trends, Osiris gives you more style variety for less money.

This is one of the most common questions that comes up during eye exams, and the answer often depends on individual factors that only a professional assessment can evaluate. What works for one person may not be ideal for another — your prescription, daily activities, facial anatomy, and budget all play a role in determining the best approach.

If you're in the Edmonton area, Charm Optical offers comprehensive eye exams that go beyond just measuring your prescription. Their optometrists assess your overall eye health, discuss your visual needs, and recommend lens and frame options tailored to your lifestyle. With direct insurance billing for most Alberta providers and same-day glasses for many prescriptions, getting the right eyewear doesn't have to be complicated or expensive. Book online — appointments are usually available within a few days.

sections.h2: How Much Do Osiris Glasses Cost?
sections.content:
CategoryFrame PriceWith Single Vision LensesWith Progressive Lenses
Basic models$100 — $140$200 — $340$350 — $540
Premium models$140 — $200$240 — $400$390 — $600

Pricing varies by retailer. Some chains include basic lenses in the frame price as part of bundle deals, which can make Osiris an especially good value.

With insurance coverage of $200-$500 every two years, Osiris frames can often be fully covered, leaving your benefit to pay for lens upgrades like anti-reflective coating or high-index material.

Pricing varies significantly depending on where you buy and what lens features you choose. In Canada, the total cost of a complete pair of glasses (frame + lenses) typically breaks down as follows: budget options start at $150-$250 for basic frames with standard single vision lenses. Mid-range options with anti-reflective coating and a name-brand frame run $300-$500. Premium designer frames with progressive or high-index lenses can reach $600-$900+. The biggest variable is usually the lens — a basic single vision lens costs far less than a digital freeform progressive with premium coatings.

Insurance makes a significant difference. Most employer health plans in Alberta cover $200-$500 toward glasses every 1-2 years. Charm Optical direct-bills most Alberta insurance providers — Alberta Blue Cross, Canada Life, Desjardins, and more — so you only pay the difference at the counter. No paperwork, no reimbursement wait. Book an eye exam to get your prescription checked and learn exactly what your plan covers before you shop.

sections.h2: Where to Buy Osiris Glasses in Canada
sections.content:
Osiris distribution depends on your region and local optical retailers:

  • Specsavers: The primary retailer for Osiris frames globally. Specsavers has expanded into Canada in recent years — check if there's a location near you.
  • Other optical chains: Some chains carry Osiris or similar in-house brands at comparable price points.
  • Not widely available online: Unlike major licensed brands, Osiris frames aren't typically sold through independent online retailers.

If Osiris isn't available at your local store, similar style-forward frames at comparable prices exist from other retail brands. Ask your optician about their in-house collections — most stores carry budget-friendly frames alongside designer options. At Charm Optical in Edmonton, the frame selection spans from affordable options to luxury brands, with opticians who can help you find trendy styles at your budget.

Whichever brand you choose, getting properly fitted lenses matters more than the frame brand. A $150 frame with perfectly centered lenses will outperform a $400 frame with inaccurate optical centres every time.

A well-fitting frame does more than look good — it ensures your lenses perform correctly. Frames that sit too high or low shift the optical centres away from your pupils, causing blurry vision and eye strain. Frames that are too wide slide down your nose, while tight frames create pressure headaches behind the ears.

Three measurements define frame fit: lens width (the horizontal size of each lens opening), bridge width (the gap between the two lenses), and temple length (the arm that hooks behind your ear). These are printed on the inside of the temple arm as three numbers like 52-18-140. Your optician uses these alongside your face measurements to recommend frames that balance aesthetics and function.

This is why in-person fitting matters, especially for progressive lenses where vertical lens alignment is critical. At Charm Optical in Edmonton, the opticians take precise measurements using digital tools to ensure your frames and lenses work together perfectly. With over 500 frames from brands like Ray-Ban, Oakley, and Versace, you'll find the right combination of style and fit. Most styles are available for same-day glasses at Charm Optical with single vision lenses.

Your eyes are one of the most important tools you have, and they deserve proper care and correction. The technology behind modern eyewear has advanced remarkably in recent years — from digital freeform progressive lenses that customize the optics to your exact wearing position, to lightweight titanium frames that weigh less than a coin, to anti-reflective coatings that virtually eliminate glare. Understanding these options empowers you to make better choices about your eye health and get glasses that genuinely improve your quality of life rather than just meeting a basic need.

Regular eye exams are the foundation of good vision care. The Canadian Association of Optometrists recommends exams every one to two years for adults and annually for children, seniors over 65, and anyone with diabetes or a family history of eye disease. A comprehensive exam does far more than measure your prescription — your optometrist checks the health of your retina, optic nerve, and blood vessels, catching conditions like glaucoma and macular degeneration in their earliest stages. Many serious eye conditions have no noticeable symptoms until significant damage has occurred, making preventive exams essential.

The best next step is always a professional eye exam. Your optometrist can evaluate your specific needs and recommend the right combination of lenses, coatings, and frames for your lifestyle and budget. In Alberta, most employer insurance plans cover comprehensive eye exams, and children under 18 and adults over 65 receive coverage through Alberta Health.

Charm Optical at 5035 Ellerslie Rd SW in South Edmonton offers thorough eye exams with experienced optometrists, a selection of over 500 designer frames from brands like Ray-Ban, Oakley, and Versace, and same-day glasses for many prescription types. They offer direct insurance billing for most Alberta providers, so you only pay the difference at the counter.

Whether you are buying your first pair of glasses, upgrading to progressive lenses, or just need an adjustment on your current frames, having a trusted local optician makes the process smoother. Book an appointment online — most appointments are available within a few days, and walk-ins are welcome for adjustments and frame browsing.

faqs.q: Is Osiris a good glasses brand?
faqs.a: Osiris offers decent quality for its $100-$200 price range. The frames use standard acetate and metal with adequate hinge construction. They're good for trend-conscious buyers who want modern styles at budget prices. For longer-term durability, mid-range brands like Ray-Ban offer better build quality.

Osiris is an in-house brand produced for optical retail chains, most notably Specsavers. It's not a licensed fashion brand — the frames are designed and manufactured specifically for retail distribution at accessible price points.

faqs.q: Where can I buy Osiris glasses in Canada?
faqs.a: Osiris is primarily available at Specsavers locations. Availability in Canada depends on whether Specsavers has expanded to your area. If not available locally, ask your optical store about similar in-house brands at comparable prices.

faqs.q: How much do Osiris glasses cost?
faqs.a: Osiris frames range from $100-$200. Complete glasses (frame + lenses) cost $200-$400 for single vision and $350-$600 for progressive lenses, depending on lens material and coatings. Some retailers include basic lenses in bundle deals.

faqs.q: How do I know my glasses frame size?
faqs.a: Look inside the temple arm for three numbers (e.g., 52-18-140). The first is lens width in mm, second is bridge width, third is temple length. If you don't have a current pair, an optician at Charm Optical can measure your face to determine the ideal frame dimensions.

faqs.q: What frame material is most durable?
faqs.a: Titanium is the most durable frame material — it's lightweight, corrosion-resistant, hypoallergenic, and flexes without breaking. For budget durability, TR-90 nylon is nearly indestructible. Acetate offers the best colour variety. Charm Optical carries all three materials across their 500+ frame collection.

ctaText: Charm Optical carries designer and affordable frames for every budget. Try on styles in store and get expert fitting advice in South Edmonton.
